response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his career is centered on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not the deep C++ systems engineering this role demands. His only professional C++ experience is a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practice. The low-latency DLang work shows systems aptitude, but the gap between his profile and a hands-on C++ architect role in industrial automation is too wide.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks, and a track record of mentoring and teaching
Broad polyglot background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts with long tenure in senior roles
concerns: Minimal professional C++/STL experience — about seven months in 2014-2015, far short of the 8-12 years of C++-centric work expected
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Recent experience is web/product-focused (Stripe, Shopify), suggesting a poor match for networked multi-threaded C++ platform development
